Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Choi" or "HOI" or "LN-2026-00621" (or similar) using search_emails to find DeShawn Choi's email to Sofia Reyes with updated docs and closing questions, DeShawn's HOI binder email initially sent to Keisha Williams's personal Gmail (keisha.williams@gmail.com) rather than her Keystone email (subject: "HOI binder for LN-2026-00621"), and DeShawn's follow-up resend of the binder to keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com. The agent discovers that the HOI binder was sent to Keisha's personal inbox rather than going through the loan file workflow, which is why it never appeared in the LOS checklist.
OE 2: Search Slack with a query related to "Choi" or "HOI" or "00621" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Sofia Reyes noting DeShawn sent docs but HOI is missing from file (channel C002), Brittany Wallace logging a call from DeShawn asking about closing status (channel C002), Keisha Williams noting "I think he sent HOI over last week but I don't see it in the file" (channel C002), and Tyler Washington's own pre-close review messages in channel C003 asking the team if anyone has the binder.
OE 3: Search the Mortgage LOS for LN-2026-00621 using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00621" or "Choi") or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00621") to retrieve the loan details. The agent discovers the loan is in "clear_to_close" status with a closing date of 2026-04-13, loan amount $288,000 (conventional purchase), and rate lock expiration 2026-04-22.
OE 4: Search CRM for engagement notes related to DeShawn Choi using crm_list_engagements (with contact_ids for crm_crmcontact_5ce112a1f90a) or by first looking up the contact via crm_search_contacts (full_name: "DeShawn Choi" or similar). The agent discovers multiple engagement notes including: "Pre-close review found HOI binder missing from file," "HOI binder still missing during pre-close," and critically: "LO said HOI binder went to personal inbox last week, not file workflow." This CRM note confirms where the binder ended up and is essential to resolving the issue.
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "LN-2026-00556" or "Turner" or "UWM conditions" (or similar) using search_emails to find Tyler's original submission email to casey.morgan@uwm.com, UWM's response (subject: "UW conditions - LN-2026-00556") listing 15 outstanding conditions including updated pay documentation, full bank statements, large deposit explanations, signed disclosures, employer start date clarification, credit inquiry letters of explanation, HOI information, earnest money documentation, title/contact details, and file cleanup items. Also discovers Elena Marchetti's coaching email (subject: "Pre-sub steps for LN-2026-00556") walking Tyler through the pre-submission checklist he missed.
OE 6: Search Slack with a query related to "00556" or "UWM kicked" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Tyler's messages in channel C002: "UWM kicked back LN-2026-00556 w/ 15 conds," his request to Elena for help, and his admission that many of the conditions are things he should have caught pre-submission.
OE 7: Search the Mortgage LOS for LN-2026-00556 using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00556") to retrieve the loan details. The agent discovers the loan has a closing date of 2026-04-12, loan amount $293,600 (conventional purchase), and assigned LO is Priya Desai (los_staff_930d770d3309).
OE 8: Search CRM for engagement notes related to LN-2026-00556 using crm_list_engagements (filtering by relevant contact_ids or listing recent engagements broadly, since some engagements such as Keisha's timing concern have empty contact_ids). The agent discovers: "Reviewed internal pre-sub checklist before coaching processor on 00556" (Elena's note) and Keisha's timing concern noting she reviewed the condition list, said at least half are items that should be clean before submission, told Tyler it adds a week minimum, and wants to sit in on his next submission as a check.
OE 9: Search emails with a query related to "Baker" or "Monique" or "LN-2026-00611" (or similar) using search_emails to find Sofia Reyes's email to Monique Baker requesting fresh documents (pay stubs, bank statements, credit re-pull authorization), Monique's angry reply calling the process "unbelievable" and threatening to take the loan to another lender, and Carlos Rivera's apology email acknowledging the delays and asking Monique to stay.
OE 10: Search Slack with a query related to "Baker" or "00611" or "Monique" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Tyler's message about setting calendar follow-ups for the Baker file (channel C002), and Sofia Reyes's message that Monique fired back and is threatening to leave (channel C002).
OE 11: Search the Mortgage LOS for LN-2026-00611 using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00611") to retrieve the loan details. The agent discovers the loan is in "processing" status with no closing date set, loan amount $214,000 (conventional purchase), assigned LO is Carlos Rivera (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd), assigned processor is Sofia Reyes (los_staff_afc9caafae9d), and rate lock expiration is 2026-04-10 (already expired as of the task date April 13, 2026).
OE 12: Search CRM for engagement notes related to Monique Baker using crm_list_engagements (with contact_ids for crm_crmcontact_aa49d46d6a91) or by first looking up the contact via crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Monique Baker" or similar). The agent discovers the engagement note: "Carlos apologized for delays and acknowledged refresh request frustration. Asked Monique to stay with file while team pushes updated docs through."
OE 13: Look up contact information for Elena Marchetti, Keisha Williams, Carlos Rivera, and Sofia Reyes using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Marchetti"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Keisha Williams"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carlos Rivera"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Sofia Reyes") to retrieve their verified email addresses: elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com, keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com, carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, and sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com. These are needed before sending emails.
OE 14: Send an email (via send_email) from tyler.washington@keystonemortgage.com to keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com requesting the HOI binder for DeShawn Choi's file LN-2026-00621 be forwarded to the loan file or closing team, since the CRM notes indicate it went to her inbox rather than the file workflow. The email should note that pre-close review identified the gap and the binder is needed to clear the pre-close check.
→ Write action → Outcome rubric (1.1/1.2)
OE 15: Add an activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity, action: "note") on LN-2026-00621 (loan_id: los_loan_dc34bc01927c) documenting that the HOI binder gap was identified during pre-close review and that the binder was traced to Keisha Williams's personal inbox rather than the loan file.
→ Write action → Outcome rubric (1.1/1.2)
OE 16: Add conditions (via mortgage_los_add_condition, condition_type: "prior_to_docs" or similar) on LN-2026-00556 (loan_id: los_loan_ae236f30d53a) for the outstanding items from UWM's underwriting response. The conditions should cover the key open items identified in the lender's 15-condition kickback (updated pay documentation, bank statements, large deposit explanations, signed disclosures, etc.).
→ Write action → Outcome rubric (1.1/1.2)
OE 17: Send an email (via send_email) from tyler.washington@keystonemortgage.com to elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com summarizing which conditions on LN-2026-00556 Tyler is picking up first, so Elena is not duplicating effort on the resubmission.
→ Write action → Outcome rubric (1.1/1.2)
OE 18: Send an email or determine the appropriate follow-up for the Baker file. If fresh documents from Monique Baker have not been received, email Carlos Rivera (carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com) or Sofia Reyes (sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com) flagging that follow-up is needed on the outstanding document request. The email should note the borrower's escalation threat.
→ Write action → Outcome rubric (1.1/1.2)
OE 19: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) documenting the current status and escalation risk on Monique Baker's file. The engagement should note the borrower's threat to move the loan elsewhere, that original documents expired after 75 days in process, and the current status of the fresh document request.
→ Write action → Outcome rubric (1.1/1.2)
OE 20: Look up Slack channel information using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel" or similar) to find the closings channel (C003, #closings) for posting the status update.
OE 21: Post a status update (via conversations_add_message) in channel C003 (#closings) covering all three files: (1) Choi LN-2026-00621, HOI binder missing from file, traced to LO inbox, pre-close on hold; (2) Turner LN-2026-00556, UWM kicked back with 15 conditions, working through resubmission; (3) Baker LN-2026-00611, documents expired, borrower upset, awaiting fresh docs.
→ Write action → Outcome rubric (1.1/1.2)

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
